title: Betla National Park
tagline: Betla National Park — wildlife reserve of Jharkhand

overview.short: Betla National Park is a national park located in the Latehar and Palamu district of Jharkhand, India.
overview.description: Betla National Park is a national park located in the Latehar and Palamu district of Jharkhand, India. Its the only national park in the state. It is spread over an area of 226.32 km2 (87.38 sq mi) and is a part of the Palamu Tiger Reserve. It was declared a national park in 1986.

topPlaces.name: Palamu fort
topPlaces.description: The Palamu Fort are two ruined forts located 3 k.m from Betla National Park, Latehar district on the bank of Auranga River, in the Indian state of Jharkhand. The original fort in the plains and the other on an adjoining hill are attributed to the kings of the Chero dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Satbarwa block
topPlaces.description: Satbarwa block is one of the administrative blocks of Palamu district, Jharkhand state, India. It is one of the important block/Taliuka in NH-75 (old numbering).

topPlaces.name: Polpol
topPlaces.description: Polpol is a village of Palamau district, situated 16 km from the nearest district town, Medininagar. National Highway 39 crosses this village.

topPlaces.name: Barwadih
topPlaces.description: Barwadih is a town and Community Development Block in Latehar district of Jharkhand state in India.

items.note: The Palamu Fort are two ruined forts located 3 k.m from Betla National Park, Latehar district on the bank of Auranga River, in the Indian state of Jharkhand. The original fort in the plains and the other on an adjoining hill are attributed to the kings of the Chero dynasty.
Satbarwa block is one of the administrative blocks of Palamu district, Jharkhand state, India. It is one of the important block/Taliuka in NH-75 (old numbering).
Polpol is a village of Palamau district, situated 16 km from the nearest district town, Medininagar. National Highway 39 crosses this village.
Barwadih is a town and Community Development Block in Latehar district of Jharkhand state in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Betla National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Betla National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Betla National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gaya International Airport (123 km) and the nearest railway station is Ranchi Junction (129 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Betla National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Betla National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Betla National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Betla National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Betla National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Palamu fort, Satbarwa block, Polpol, Barwadih.
